coastal.title: Homes, structures destroyed as B.C. wildfires burn out of control
coastal.description: In the Fraser Canyon, where three massive fires, called the Brunswick Creek Complex, are burning, the past few days have been heartbreaking for a farm owner in North Bend.

coastal.title: Pyrocumulus clouds from wildfire in Fraser Canyon loom high over B.C.
coastal.description: BC Wildfire Service said the Anderson Creek fire was discovered by aircraft responding to one of the other nearby fires on Tuesday and it quickly flared up, fanned by strong winds.

kamloops.title: Aggressive wildfire near Clinton forces expanded evacuation order, alert
kamloops.description: There are now approximately 193 properties on evacuation order and 104 properties on evacuation alert due to the Fiftynine Creek wildfire in the Thompson Nicola Regional District.

all.title: Canada wildfire smoke will cover more US states soon, forecasts show
all.description: Communities within the United States’ Great Lakes region will experience their highest air pollution levels throughout Sunday due to wildfire smoke from Canada, but National Weather Service forecasts show significant relief isn’t coming anytime soon. Smoke has lowered air quality across the Midwest and Northeast to unhealthy and even hazardous levels over the past week as numerous [&#8

all.title: Canada wildfire totals explode to historic highs, US officials warn to extinguish fires ‘as fast as possible’
all.description: What began as a far-below-average wildfire season for Canada has since exploded past historical norms, causing national crews to mobilize and United States officials to issue warnings. Area burned in Canada was less than half of the 10-year average on July 7, but just 10 days and over 300 wildfires later, area burned now sits […]

all.title: Canada wildfire smoke blankets US skies across the Midwest and Northeast, triggering health alerts
all.description: Air quality levels are forecast to drop to unhealthy levels across the U.S. Midwest and Northeast on Tuesday as smoke from Canadian wildfires intensifies. AirNow’s Fire and Smoke Map showed the regions blanketed in yellow due to the drifting smoke. Winds from the northwest are pushing smoke into the region, with National Weather Service forecasters […]
